definitions.technical: An attack in which an adversary controlling more than half of a proof-of-work network's hash rate (or equivalent resource in other consensus models) can unilaterally determine the canonical chain, enabling double-spending and transaction censorship. The 51% threshold applies specifically to Nakamoto-style longest-chain consensus; BFT systems have a lower tolerance threshold of one-third.
definitions.lay: When a single entity gains control of more than half the computing power on a blockchain, it can rewrite recent transactions and spend the same coins twice. This is the fundamental security assumption of proof-of-work blockchains: as long as no one controls the majority, the system is safe.
definitions.parenthetical: (an attack where a single entity controlling majority network resources can rewrite transaction history)
